parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.value: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

parameters.text:
=You are a metal music curator. A listener's taste profile:
- Favorite bands: {{ $json.favBands.join(', ') }}
- Favorite genres: {{ $json.favGenres.join(', ') }}
- NOT interested in: {{ $json.notGenres.join(', ') }}

Here are the new releases to evaluate:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.releases) }}

Return ONLY a JSON array of the releases this listener would genuinely enjoy. For each, include:
- id (original id from input)
- artist
- track
- genre
- country
- year
- sourceUrl
- imageUrl
- description (2 sentences, enthusiastic, why they'd like it)

If none are relevant, return an empty array [].
Return raw JSON only, no markdown, no explanation.

nodes.name: Send Favorite Alert
nodes.notes: Replace YOUR_CHAT_ID below with your actual Telegram chat ID

parameters.text:
=The user sent this message to their music bot:
"{{ $json.message.text }}"

The bot manages three lists:
- favBands: favorite artist/band names
- favGenres: favorite music genres
- notGenres: genres they dislike

Parse the user's intent and return ONLY this JSON, no markdown, no explanation:
{
  "action": "add" | "remove" | "list" | "unknown",
  "listType": "favBands" | "favGenres" | "notGenres" | null,
  "value": "the band or genre name, normalized" | null,
  "reply": "a friendly short confirmation message to send back to the user"
}

Examples:
- "add band Spiritbox" → action:add, listType:favBands, value:"Spiritbox"
- "remove genre Nu Metal" → action:remove, listType:notGenres, value:"Nu Metal"
- "I don't like Rap Metal" → action:add, listType:notGenres, value:"Rap Metal"
- "add Lorna Shore to my favorites" → action:add, listType:favBands, value:"Lorna Shore"
- "show my favorites" → action:list, listType:null
- "what bands are you tracking" → action:list, listType:null
